remove.reason: EXACT DUPLICATE - Rules are doing the same thing. aws.bedrock.model.bedrock_artifact_encrypted_at_rest has equal compliance attachments (0 vs 0). These should not both exist.

keep.reason: More comprehensive - checks 6 fields (includes all 1 fields from aws.bedrock.model.bedrock_image_scan_passed_configured). Subset relationships are acceptable, but exact duplicates are not.

remove.reason: Field subset - checks only 1 of 6 fields. aws.bedrock.resource.model_invocation_logging_enabled is more comprehensive. This is a subset relationship (acceptable), not an exact duplicate.

aws.bedrock.model.bedrock_artifact_encry.metadata_intent: Check that custom models have KMS encryption enabled (modelKmsKeyArn exists)
issues_found: Discovery get_custom_model was used without required modelIdentifier parameter
Missing list_custom_models discovery to enumerate models first
fixes_applied: Added list_custom_models discovery to enumerate all custom models
Made get_custom_model dependent on list_custom_models with modelIdentifier parameter

aws.bedrock.model.bedrock_kms_cmk_used_c.metadata_intent: Check that custom models use KMS CMK (modelKmsKeyArn exists)

aws.bedrock.model.kms_encryption_enabled.metadata_intent: Check that models use KMS customer managed keys (modelKmsKeyArn exists)
issues_found: Same discovery issue as other KMS rules
metadata_mapping suggests get_imported_model but YAML uses get_custom_model (both require params)
fixes_applied: Note: Only checks custom models, not imported models (would need separate discovery)

aws.bedrock.resource.model_invocation_lo.metadata_intent: Check that model invocation logging is enabled with all delivery types
aws.bedrock.resource.model_invocation_lo.yaml_checks: Checks cloudWatchConfig and s3Config exist, and all data delivery types enabled
issues_found: Operator error: exists with value 'true' should be exists with null for object checks
fixes_applied: Fixed operator: changed exists with value 'true' to exists with value null for cloudWatchConfig and s3Config

aws.bedrock.resource.model_invocation_lo.metadata_intent: Check that model invocation logs encryption is enabled
fixes_applied: Fixed operator: changed exists with value 'true' to exists with value null

issues_found: ResourceNotFoundException expected when no guardrails configured
fixes_applied: Added on_error: continue to handle ResourceNotFoundException gracefully

aws.bedrock.model.execution_role_least_p.yaml_checks: Checks roleArn exists (only verifies role is configured, not least privilege)
issues_found: Only checks role existence, not actual least privilege permissions
metadata_mapping suggests get_model_invocation_job but YAML uses list_model_invocation_jobs (correct for discovery)
aws.bedrock.model.execution_role_least_p.note: Limitation: Cannot verify least privilege without analyzing IAM policy. Current check only verifies role is configured.
